message:
fix(alloc,fuse,mvcc): correct bitmap OOB sentinel, unignore stable E2E tests, enrich SSI conflict logging

ffs-alloc: bitmap_get now returns true (allocated) for out-of-bounds
indices instead of false (free). Treating OOB bitmap positions as free
was incorrect -- an out-of-range bit index should indicate the block is
unavailable, not available for allocation. This prevents the allocator
from handing out blocks beyond the bitmap's coverage.

ffs-harness: Remove #[ignore = "requires /dev/fuse"] from 5 lightweight
FUSE E2E tests that have been validated as stable in the current
environment: fuse_create_and_read_file, fuse_mkdir_and_nested_create,
fuse_unlink_removes_file, btrfs_fuse_readdir_root, and
btrfs_fuse_create_and_read_file. Update the module-level doc comment to
reflect that both read-only and lightweight rw tests now run by default.
Heavier write-path tests remain gated behind --ignored.

ffs-mvcc: Enrich the SSI (Serializable Snapshot Isolation) conflict
debug log with two additional fields -- writer_snapshot_high and
writer_read_set_size -- to aid diagnosis of serialization failures. When
a read-write conflict is detected, knowing the conflicting writer's
snapshot boundary and read set cardinality helps distinguish between
genuine anti-dependency cycles and false positives from conservative
conflict detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
